question: Does the generated image include a sika deer with antlers and a spotted coat pattern consistent with the reference image?
Is the photographer wearing a green hooded jacket, black pants, and a hiking backpack consistent with the reference image?
Are the photographer's facial features (e.g., beard, face shape) consistent with the reference image?
Does the tripod match the reference image in design (black carbon fiber legs, fluid head)?
Is the background consistent with the reference forest clearing, featuring ferns and dappled sunlight?
Is the tripod positioned in the left foreground of the composition?
Is the photographer positioned in the left mid-ground area?
Is the deer positioned in the right mid-ground area?
Are the subjects scaled realistically for their depth (Tripod largest, then Photographer, then Deer)?
Is the photographer depicted in a crouching or half-crouching pose?
Is the photographer looking through the camera viewfinder with the lens aimed toward the deer?
Do the tripod feet show visible indentation shadows or signs of pressing into the grass?
Is the tripod partially occluded by foreground fern fronds to add spatial depth?
Are dappled light patterns (sunlight spots) visible on the photographer's clothing?
Are dappled light patterns visible on the deer's body?
Are dappled light patterns visible on the tripod?
Are the mid-ground subjects (photographer and deer) rendered with sharp focus?
Is the background forest rendered with significant blur compared to the mid-ground?
Is the foreground foliage slightly blurred to create depth?
Are there any obvious anatomical distortions on the photographer (e.g., extra limbs, malformed hands)?
Are there any obvious anatomical distortions on the deer (e.g., extra legs, malformed antlers)?
Are there any structural distortions on the tripod (e.g., warped legs, missing components)?
Do the subjects appear naturally blended into the scene without obvious 'pasted-on' edges or halos?
Does the image exhibit a subtle natural noise/grain consistent with the requested realistic style?
